thesis: Microsoft is the dominant enterprise cloud platform with Azure growing 40% YoY and an AI business already exceeding $10B annual run rate. At ~22x P/E with $281.7B revenue and $101.8B net income, the valuation has compressed meaningfully while fundamentals remain best-in-class. The $365 price offers a 35% discount to analyst consensus targets, creating a clear positive risk/reward for the world's most durable software franchise.

drivers: AI monetization accelerates beyond $10B ARR into $20B+ by FY2027
Azure sustains 35%+ growth as enterprise AI workloads scale
Operating margins expand 200-300bps via AI-driven pricing power and efficiency gains

risks: AI capex overshoot leads to supply glut and margin compression
Azure growth decelerates to sub-20% as hyperscaler competition intensifies
Multiple compresses to 18x on fears of AI ROI disappointment

base_case.assumptions: Azure grows 25-30% through FY2027, Microsoft Cloud sustains 20%+ growth, operating margins stable at 44-45%, P/E re-rates to 26-28x on AI execution.

catalysts.event: Q4 FY2026 earnings — Azure growth trajectory and AI revenue disclosure
Ignite 2026 — AI product roadmap and Copilot monetization updates
Q1 FY2027 earnings — first full quarter with new AI capacity online

key_risks: Hyperscaler capex arms race may overshoot demand, pressuring ROI timelines
Regulatory scrutiny on AI dominance and cloud market power in EU and US
Open-source AI models commoditize LLM layer, eroding Azure AI pricing power
